pytorch_task_v1.prompt_instructions: Implement this ML task as a SINGLE self-contained Python file (task.py). Include all required functions AND a complete `if __name__ == '__main__':` block that trains the model, evaluates on a validation split, prints standard metrics (MSE, R2, parameter accuracy where applicable), and asserts quality thresholds so the script exits non-zero on failure. Do NOT create separate test files or README. The script itself is the test.
evaluation_rules: The `evaluate()` function MUST compute and return standard metrics: MSE, R2 score, and any task-specific metrics on the VALIDATION data split.

The `if __name__ == '__main__':` block MUST: (1) train the model, (2) evaluate on BOTH train and validation splits, (3) print all metrics clearly, (4) assert quality thresholds (e.g., R2 > 0.9, MSE < threshold), (5) exit 0 on success or non-zero on failure.
Do NOT use a separate test file. All evaluation logic goes in main().

tasks.description: Implement exponential smoothing (single and double) from scratch using PyTorch tensors. No autograd or optimizer—use grid search over alpha/beta to select best params. Establish baseline MSE on a simple univariate series.

requirements.math: Docstring must contain the exponential smoothing recurrence: s_t = alpha * x_t + (1-alpha) * s_{t-1}.
requirements.data: Synthetic: sinusoid + trend + Gaussian noise. 80% train / 20% validation split.
requirements.implementation: Manual update loop; no torch.nn, no torch.optim. Grid search over alpha (and beta for double) to minimize train MSE; update model with best params. Return smoothed series and one-step-ahead forecasts.
requirements.evaluation: evaluate() must compute MSE, MAE, R2 on validation one-step forecasts.
requirements.main_block: Print learned alpha/beta, train/val MSE; assert MSE < variance of series.
requirements.output: Save learned params (alpha, beta, use_double) with torch.save() to ts_lvl1_params.pt; metrics to ts_lvl1_metrics.json.

tasks.description: Implement an AR(p) model using nn.Linear to predict next value from past p values. Use autograd and Adam optimizer.

requirements.math: Define AR(p): x_t = sum_i w_i * x_{t-i} + bias in LaTeX.
requirements.data: Synthetic AR(2) process: x_t = 0.6*x_{t-1} + 0.3*x_{t-2} + noise. Create sliding-window features [x_{t-p}, ..., x_{t-1}] -> x_t. 80% train / 20% validation split.
requirements.evaluation: MSE, MAE, R2 on validation; assert R2 > 0.6.
requirements.output: Save model state_dict with torch.save() to ts_lvl2_model.pt; metrics (loss_history, val_mse, val_mae, val_r2) to ts_lvl2_metrics.json.

tasks.description: Implement LSTM-based sequence-to-sequence forecasting for multi-step ahead predictions. Use teacher forcing during training; support configurable horizon.

requirements.math: Explain LSTM cell equations or multi-step prediction setup in docstring.
requirements.data: Synthetic: sum of sinusoids + noise. Normalize; create sequences of length seq_len -> horizon. 80% train / 20% validation split.
requirements.implementation: nn.LSTM; Adam; LR scheduler (ReduceLROnPlateau); gradient clipping; early stopping on val loss.
requirements.visualization: Save 'ts_lvl3_multistep.png' showing true vs predicted for horizons 1, 3, 5.
requirements.evaluation: MSE per horizon; assert horizon-1 MSE improves over naive (last-seen) baseline.
requirements.output: Save model state_dict with torch.save() to ts_lvl3_model.pt; metrics (loss_history, mse_per_horizon, naive_mse_h1) to ts_lvl3_metrics.json.

tasks.description: Add Monte Carlo dropout for prediction intervals; compare to point forecasts. Include EDA, preprocessing, and JSON output suitable for production reporting.

requirements.data: Synthetic: sinusoid + trend + Gaussian noise. Normalize; create sequences of length seq_len for one-step-ahead prediction. 80% train / 20% validation split.
requirements.eda: Plot series (first 500 points) and simple lag correlation; save to ts_lvl4_eda.png.
requirements.implementation: LSTM with dropout; MC dropout at inference (N forward passes); combine MC std with residual-based scaling for calibrated 90% intervals.
requirements.evaluation: MSE, MAE; coverage of 90% prediction interval (fraction of true values inside interval); assert coverage in [0.8, 0.98].
requirements.output: Save model state_dict with torch.save() to ts_lvl4_model.pt; report (point_metrics, interval_coverage, interval_width_mean, loss_history) to ts_lvl4_report.json; save forecast_with_intervals.png.
requirements.validation: MC dropout intervals have reasonable coverage via residual calibration.
